get.description: Retrieves all Server records stored in the System.
get.x-permissions: Depends on the Resource access rights.

parameters.description: Server-specific key-value parameters.

flags.description:
Possible values are one of or the combination by `|` of the following: - `"SF_None"` - `"SF_Edge"` - `"SF_RemoteEC"` - `"SF_HasPublicIP"` - `"SF_IfListCtrl"` - `"SF_ArmServer"` The Server has a setting isArmServer=true. - `"SF_NewSystem"`

Deprecated. Is returned by the API only for compatibility. A proper indicator of a new System is the empty (null) localSystemId.

The System is just installed, it has the default admin password and is not connected to the Cloud. - `"SF_SupportsTranscoding"` - `"SF_HasPoeManagementCapability"` Server can provide information about built-in PoE block. - `"SF_HasFanMonitoringCapability"` Server can provide information about internal fan and its state. - `"SF_HasBuzzer"` Server has a buzzer that can produce sound. - `"SF_AdminApiForPowerUsers"`

locationId.description: If `isFailoverEnabled` is true then Devices are moved across the Servers with the same `locationId`.

backupBitrateBytesPerSecond.description:
Backup bitrate per day of week and hour, as a JSON array of key-value objects, structured according to the following example: 

[
    {
        "key": { "day": "DAY_OF_WEEK", "hour": HOUR },
        "value": BYTES_PER_SECOND
    },
    ...
]
Here DAY_OF_WEEK is one of monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, sunday; HOUR is an integer in range 0..23; BYTES_PER_SECOND is an integer that can be represented as a number or a string.
For any day-hour position, a missing value means "unlimited bitrate", and a zero value means "don't perform backup".

status.description:
Possible values are:
- `"Offline"` The Device is inaccessible.
- `"Unauthorized"` The Device does not have correct credentials in the database.
- `"Online"` The Device is operating normally.
- `"Recording"` Applies only to Cameras. The Camera is online and recording the video stream.
- `"NotDefined"` The Device status is unknown. It may show up while Servers synchronize status information.
If this status persists, it indicates an internal System problem.
- `"Incompatible"` Applies only to Servers. The Server is incompatible only when it has the System name
different from the current one, or it has an incompatible protocol version.
- `"mismatchedCertificate"` Applies only to Servers. Server is in this state if its DB certificate doesn't match the
certificate provided during the SSL handshake on a Server-to-Server connection. This may
happen in the case of a man-in-the-middle attack, or in the case of a certificate loss on
the target Server.

parameters.description: Storage-specific key-value parameters.

type.description:
Type of the method to access the Storage.

Possible values are:
- `"local"`
- `"network"` Manually mounted NAS.
- `"smb"` Auto mounted NAS.

spaceLimitB.description:
Free space to maintain on the Storage, in bytes. Recommended value is 10 gigabytes for local
Storage, and 100 gigabytes for NAS.

isUsedForWriting.description: Whether writing to the Storage is allowed.

isBackup.description: Whether the Storage is used for backup.

Flag to filter out Server Info from offline Servers. Use this flag
    together with "_local" flag to control request propagation and response details.

serverFlags.description:
Possible values are one of or the combination by `|` of the following: - `"SF_None"` - `"SF_Edge"` - `"SF_RemoteEC"` - `"SF_HasPublicIP"` - `"SF_IfListCtrl"` - `"SF_ArmServer"` The Server has a setting isArmServer=true. - `"SF_NewSystem"`

Deprecated. Is returned by the API only for compatibility. A proper indicator of a new System is the empty (null) localSystemId.

The System is just installed, it has the default admin password and is not connected to the Cloud. - `"SF_SupportsTranscoding"` - `"SF_HasPoeManagementCapability"` Server can provide information about built-in PoE block. - `"SF_HasFanMonitoringCapability"` Server can provide information about internal fan and its state. - `"SF_HasBuzzer"` Server has a buzzer that can produce sound. - `"SF_AdminApiForPowerUsers"`

synchronizedTimeMs.description: Current time synchronized with the VMS System, in milliseconds since epoch.

cloudOwnerId.description: Presented if the System is bound to the Cloud.

saasState.description:
Possible values are:
- `"uninitialized"` Saas services is not loaded.
- `"active"` Saas services is active.
- `"suspended"` Saas services are suspended manually.
- `"shutdown"` Saas services are shut down manually.
- `"autoShutdown"` Saas services are shut down automatically.

remoteAddresses.description: If any of these addresses don't contain port, ModuleInformation::port must be used.

userProvidedCertificatePem.description:
The content of a user-provided file `cert.pem` on the Server file system in PEM format
without private key. Empty if not provided or can't be loaded and parsed. It is used by
the Server if no Server-specific SNI is required by a Client.

certificatePem.description:
Server-generated self-signed certificate in PEM format without private key. It is used by
the Server if Server-specific SNI is required by a Client or there is no the user-provided
certificate or the user-provided certificate is invalid.

post.description:
Detaches the Server specified by {id} from the System. This means that:
- The Server local System id will be reset.
- The Server admin password will be set to default.
- All Cloud Users will be deleted from the Server DB (if any).
- The Server will be unbound from the Cloud (if it was bound).
- The Server will be disconnected from all the other Servers in the System.

post.description: Resets the Server specified by {id} to the initial state, i.e. deletes the Server database. The Server will restart after executing this command.

get.description: Retrieves all Storage records stored in the System.

parameters.name: serverId
parameters.description:
Server id. Can be obtained from "id" field via
`GET /rest/v1/servers` or be `this` to refer to the current Server, or be omitted by
using '*' placeholder to use all Servers.
Storage-specific key-value parameters.

type.description:
Type of the method to access the Storage.

Possible values are:
- `"local"`
- `"network"` Manually mounted NAS.
- `"smb"` Auto mounted NAS.

spaceLimitB.description:
Free space to maintain on the Storage, in bytes. Recommended value is 10 gigabytes for local
Storage, and 100 gigabytes for NAS.

isUsedForWriting.description: Whether writing to the Storage is allowed.

isBackup.description: Whether the Storage is used for backup.

status.description:
Possible values are:
- `"Offline"` The Device is inaccessible.
- `"Unauthorized"` The Device does not have correct credentials in the database.
- `"Online"` The Device is operating normally.
- `"Recording"` Applies only to Cameras. The Camera is online and recording the video stream.
- `"NotDefined"` The Device status is unknown. It may show up while Servers synchronize status information.
If this status persists, it indicates an internal System problem.
- `"Incompatible"` Applies only to Servers. The Server is incompatible only when it has the System name
different from the current one, or it has an incompatible protocol version.
- `"mismatchedCertificate"` Applies only to Servers. Server is in this state if its DB certificate doesn't match the
certificate provided during the SSL handshake on a Server-to-Server connection. This may
happen in the case of a man-in-the-middle attack, or in the case of a certificate loss on
the target Server.

get.description: Retrieves all Device records stored in the System.

parameters.description: Device specific key-value parameters.

id.description: Depends on `physicalId`. Calculated automatically by default.

physicalId.description: Depends on the Device hardware and firmware. Unique per device.

typeId.description: Device Driver id, can be obtained from GET /rest/v1/devices/*/types.

serverId.description: Parent Server, can be obtained from `GET /rest/v1/servers`, current Server by default.

credentials.description:
The password is only visible for admins if exposeDeviceCredentials option is enabled
in the System settings.

failoverPriority.description:
Priority for the Camera to be moved to another Server for failover (if the current Server
fails).

Possible values are:
- `"Never"` Will never be moved to another Server.
- `"Low"` Low priority against other Cameras.
- `"Medium"` Medium priority against other Cameras.
- `"High"` High priority against other cameras.

backupQuality.description: Combination (via "|") of the flags defining backup options.

backupContentType.description: Defines content to backup. It is the set of flags.

startTime.description: Time of day when the backup starts (in seconds passed from 00:00:00).

endTime.description: Time of day when the backup ends (in seconds passed from 00:00:00).

dayOfWeek.description:
Weekday for the recording task.

Possible values are:
- `1` Monday
- `2` Tuesday
- `3` Wednesday
- `4` Thursday
- `5` Friday
- `6` Saturday
- `7` Sunday

recordingType.description:
When to record.

Possible values are:
- `"always"` Record always.
- `"metadataOnly"` Record only when motion was detected.
- `"never"` Don't record.
- `"metadataAndLowQuality"` Record LQ stream always and HQ stream only on motion.

metadataTypes.description:
Types of metadata, which should trigger recording in case of corresponding
`recordingType` values.

minArchiveDays.description:
Deprecated. This fields is deprecated and used for backward compatibility only. Use 'minArchivePeriodS' instead.

Minimum number of days to keep the archive for.

maxArchiveDays.description:
Deprecated. This fields is deprecated and used for backward compatibility only. Use 'maxArchivePeriodS' instead.

Maximum number of days to keep the archive for.

minArchivePeriodS.description:
Minimum number of seconds to keep the archive for. If the value is less than or
equal to zero, it is not used.

maxArchivePeriodS.description:
Maximum number of seconds to keep the archive for. If the value is less than or
equal zero, it is not used.

type.description:
Type of motion detection method.

Possible values are:
- `"default"` Use default method.
- `"hardware"` Use motion detection grid implemented by the camera.
- `"software"` Use motion detection grid implemented by the server.
- `"window"` Use motion detection window implemented by the camera.
- `"none"` Do not perform motion detection.

status.description:
Possible values are:
- `"Offline"` The Device is inaccessible.
- `"Unauthorized"` The Device does not have correct credentials in the database.
- `"Online"` The Device is operating normally.
- `"Recording"` Applies only to Cameras. The Camera is online and recording the video stream.
- `"NotDefined"` The Device status is unknown. It may show up while Servers synchronize status information.
If this status persists, it indicates an internal System problem.
- `"Incompatible"` Applies only to Servers. The Server is incompatible only when it has the System name
different from the current one, or it has an incompatible protocol version.
- `"mismatchedCertificate"` Applies only to Servers. Server is in this state if its DB certificate doesn't match the
certificate provided during the SSL handshake on a Server-to-Server connection. This may
happen in the case of a man-in-the-middle attack, or in the case of a certificate loss on
the target Server.

capabilities.description:
Possible values are one of or the combination by `|` of the following:
- `"noCapabilities"`
- `"primaryStreamSoftMotion"` The Device primary stream is suitable for software motion detection.
- `"inputPort"` The Device has input port(s).
- `"outputPort"` The Device has output port(s).
- `"shareIp"` Whether multiple instances on the same IP address are allowed.
- `"audioTransmit"` The Device has a Two-Way Audio support.
- `"remoteArchive"` The Device supports remote archiving.
- `"setUserPassword"` Password on the Device can be changed.
- `"isDefaultPassword"` The Device has the default password now.
- `"isOldFirmware"` The Device firmware is too old.
- `"customMediaUrl"` The Device `streamUrls` field is editable.
- `"isPlaybackSpeedSupported"` The Device is an NVR which supports playback speeds 1, 2, 4, etc. natively.
- `"synchronousChannel"` The Device is one of the NVR channels that depend on each other and can be played back only
synchronously.
- `"dualStreamingForLiveOnly"`
- `"customMediaPorts"` The ports of the Device media streams are editable.
- `"absoluteTimestamps"` The Device sends absolute timestamps in the media stream.
- `"fixedQuality"` The Device does not allow to change stream quality/FPS.
- `"multicastStreaming"` The Device supports multicast streaming.
- `"boundToServer"` The Device is bound to a particular Server.
- `"dontAutoOpen"` The Server should not open video from the Device at its will.
- `"noAnalytics"` Analytics Engines should not bind to the Device.
- `"isOnvif"` The Device is initialized as an ONVIF device.
- `"isIntercom"` The Device is an intercom.

post.description: Creates a record in the System for the new Device. This method does not check for the Device availability; use POST /rest/v1/devices/*/searches with `mode` equal to `addFoundDevices` whenever possible instead.

status.description:
Possible values are:
- `"Offline"` The Device is inaccessible.
- `"Unauthorized"` The Device does not have correct credentials in the database.
- `"Online"` The Device is operating normally.
- `"Recording"` Applies only to Cameras. The Camera is online and recording the video stream.
- `"NotDefined"` The Device status is unknown. It may show up while Servers synchronize status information.

- `"Incompatible"` Applies only to Servers. The Server is incompatible only when it has the System name
different from the current one, or it has an incompatible protocol version.
- `"mismatchedCertificate"` Applies only to Servers. Server is in this state if its DB certificate doesn't match the
certificate provided during the SSL handshake on a Server-to-Server connection. This may
happen in the case of a man-in-the-middle attack, or in the case of a certificate loss on
the target Server.

- `"remoteArchive"` The Device supports remote archiving.
- `"setUserPassword"` Password on the Device can be changed.
- `"isDefaultPassword"` The Device has the default password now.
- `"isOldFirmware"` The Device firmware is too old.
- `"customMediaUrl"` The Device `streamUrls` field is editable.
- `"isPlaybackSpeedSupported"` The Device is an NVR which supports playback speeds 1, 2, 4, etc. natively.
- `"synchronousChannel"` The Device is one of the NVR channels that depend on each other and can be played back only

- `"dualStreamingForLiveOnly"`
- `"customMediaPorts"` The ports of the Device media streams are editable.
- `"absoluteTimestamps"` The Device sends absolute timestamps in the media stream.

- `"multicastStreaming"` The Device supports multicast streaming.
- `"boundToServer"` The Device is bound to a particular Server.
- `"dontAutoOpen"` The Server should not open video from the Device at its will.
- `"noAnalytics"` Analytics Engines should not bind to the Device.
- `"isOnvif"` The Device is initialized as an ONVIF device.
- `"isIntercom"` The Device is an intercom.

equal to zero, it is not used.

maxArchivePeriodS.description:
Maximum number of seconds to keep the archive for. If the value is less than or
equal zero, it is not used.

type.description:
Type of motion detection method.

Possible values are:
- `"default"` Use default method.
- `"hardware"` Use motion detection grid implemented by the camera.
- `"software"` Use motion detection grid implemented by the server.
- `"window"` Use motion detection window implemented by the camera.
- `"none"` Do not perform motion detection.

status.description:
Possible values are:
- `"Offline"` The Device is inaccessible.
- `"Unauthorized"` The Device does not have correct credentials in the database.
- `"Online"` The Device is operating normally.
- `"Recording"` Applies only to Cameras. The Camera is online and recording the video stream.
- `"NotDefined"` The Device status is unknown. It may show up while Servers synchronize status information.
If this status persists, it indicates an internal System problem.
- `"Incompatible"` Applies only to Servers. The Server is incompatible only when it has the System name
different from the current one, or it has an incompatible protocol version.
- `"mismatchedCertificate"` Applies only to Servers. Server is in this state if its DB certificate doesn't match the
certificate provided during the SSL handshake on a Server-to-Server connection. This may
happen in the case of a man-in-the-middle attack, or in the case of a certificate loss on
the target Server.

capabilities.description:
Possible values are one of or the combination by `|` of the following:
- `"noCapabilities"`
- `"primaryStreamSoftMotion"` The Device primary stream is suitable for software motion detection.
- `"inputPort"` The Device has input port(s).
- `"outputPort"` The Device has output port(s).
- `"shareIp"` Whether multiple instances on the same IP address are allowed.
- `"audioTransmit"` The Device has a Two-Way Audio support.
- `"remoteArchive"` The Device supports remote archiving.
- `"setUserPassword"` Password on the Device can be changed.
- `"isDefaultPassword"` The Device has the default password now.
- `"isOldFirmware"` The Device firmware is too old.
- `"customMediaUrl"` The Device `streamUrls` field is editable.
- `"isPlaybackSpeedSupported"` The Device is an NVR which supports playback speeds 1, 2, 4, etc. natively.
- `"synchronousChannel"` The Device is one of the NVR channels that depend on each other and can be played back only
synchronously.
- `"dualStreamingForLiveOnly"`
- `"customMediaPorts"` The ports of the Device media streams are editable.
- `"absoluteTimestamps"` The Device sends absolute timestamps in the media stream.
- `"fixedQuality"` The Device does not allow to change stream quality/FPS.
- `"multicastStreaming"` The Device supports multicast streaming.
- `"boundToServer"` The Device is bound to a particular Server.
- `"dontAutoOpen"` The Server should not open video from the Device at its will.
- `"noAnalytics"` Analytics Engines should not bind to the Device.
- `"isOnvif"` The Device is initialized as an ONVIF device.
- `"isIntercom"` The Device is an intercom.

typeId.description: Device Driver id, can be obtained from GET /rest/v1/devices/*/types.

serverId.description: Parent Server, can be obtained from `GET /rest/v1/servers`, current Server by default.

credentials.description:
The password is only visible for admins if exposeDeviceCredentials option is enabled
in the System settings.

failoverPriority.description:
Priority for the Camera to be moved to another Server for failover (if the current Server
fails).

Possible values are:
- `"Never"` Will never be moved to another Server.
- `"Low"` Low priority against other Cameras.
- `"Medium"` Medium priority against other Cameras.
- `"High"` High priority against other cameras.

backupQuality.description: Combination (via "|") of the flags defining backup options.

backupContentType.description: Defines content to backup. It is the set of flags.

startTime.description: Time of day when the backup starts (in seconds passed from 00:00:00).

endTime.description: Time of day when the backup ends (in seconds passed from 00:00:00).

dayOfWeek.description:
Weekday for the recording task.

Possible values are:
- `1` Monday
- `2` Tuesday
- `3` Wednesday
- `4` Thursday
- `5` Friday
- `6` Saturday
- `7` Sunday

recordingType.description:
When to record.

Possible values are:
- `"always"` Record always.
- `"metadataOnly"` Record only when motion was detected.
- `"never"` Don't record.
- `"metadataAndLowQuality"` Record LQ stream always and HQ stream only on motion.

metadataTypes.description:
Types of metadata, which should trigger recording in case of corresponding
`recordingType` values.

get.description: Retrieves all User records stored in the System.

email.description: For cloud users this should be equal name.

type.description:
Only local users are supposed to be created by API.

Possible values are:
- `"local"` This User is managed by VMS.
- `"temporaryLocal"` This User is managed by VMS and has a time limited access by the session token.
- `"ldap"` This User is imported from LDAP Server.
- `"cloud"` This is a Cloud User with access to this VMS System.

isHttpDigestEnabled.description:
Whether HTTP digest authorization can be used for this user. Requires password to enable.
Forbidden to change for the Cloud users as their digest support is controlled by the Cloud.

isOwner.description:
Proprietary.

Intended for internal use; keep the value when saving a previously received object, use false when creating a new one.

permissions.description:
Flags describing global user capabilities, independently of resources. Stored in the database.
QFlags uses int internally, so we are limited to 32 bits.

Possible values are one of or the combination by `|` of the following:
- `"NoGlobalPermissions"` Only live video access.
- `"GlobalAdminPermission"` Can edit other non-admins.
- `"GlobalEditCamerasPermission"` Can edit Device settings.
- `"GlobalControlVideoWallPermission"` Can control video-walls.
- `"GlobalViewLogsPermission"` Can access Event Log and Audit Trail.
- `"GlobalViewArchivePermission"` Can view archives of the available cameras.
- `"GlobalExportPermission"` Can export archives of the available cameras.
- `"GlobalViewBookmarksPermission"` Can view Bookmarks on the available cameras.
- `"GlobalManageBookmarksPermission"` Can modify Bookmarks on the available cameras.
- `"GlobalUserInputPermission"` Can change the camera PTZ state, use 2-way audio and I/O buttons.
- `"GlobalAccessAllMediaPermission"` Has access to all media Resources (cameras and web pages).
- `"GlobalCustomUserPermission"` Just marks the new user as "custom".

userRoleId.description: User Role id, can be obtained from `GET /rest/v1/userRoles`.

accessibleResources.description:
List of accessible Resource ids for the User. The access rights are calculated from the
`permissions` value when the User accesses a Resource.

get.description: Retrieves all License records stored in the System.

key.description: License serial number. Corresponds to the License Block SERIAL property.

licenseBlock.description:
License Block - a plain-text "property" file (with "key=value" lines). It is generated by the License Server, is bound to the particular Server (via Hardware Id), and is signed (the signature is stored in one of the properties). The License Block contains the following properties (keys):
  • NAME VMS product name.
  • SERIAL Serial number of the License.
  • HWID Hardware Id of the Server to which the License is bound.
  • COUNT Number of cameras which are covered by the License.
  • CLASS Type of the License. Defines which VMS platforms can use the License, and for what kind of camera-like devices.
  • VERSION VMS version.
  • BRAND VMS product name.
  • EXPIRATION Expiration date in format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. Optional; if omitted, the License lifetime is unlimited.
  • SIGNATURE2 Digital signature created by the License Server.
  • COMPANY VMS vendor.
Additionally, other proprietary keys may appear in the License Block.

cellSpacing.description:
Cell spacing between layout items as a share of an item's size
(floating-point, 0..1).

left.description: Left coordinate of the layout item (floating-point).

top.description: Top coordinate of the layout item (floating-point).

right.description: Right coordinate of the layout item (floating-point).

bottom.description: Bottom coordinate of the layout item (floating-point).

rotation.description:
Degree of image tilt; a positive value rotates counter-clockwise
(floating-point, 0..360).

resourcePath.description:
If the item represents a local file - the URL of the file. For cross-System
layouts, contains the special URL of the Device. Otherwise is empty. Can be filled with the
Device logical id when saving the Layout.

of the image width (floating-point, 0..1).

of the image height (floating-point, 0..1).

fraction of the image width (floating-point, 0..1).

fraction of the image height (floating-point, 0..1).

values in the request body. The existing settings can be obtained in the same format using
the "Get System settings" function. The complete list of settings with their types is
returned by the "Get System settings manifest" function.

setting values can be obtained using the "Get System settings" function as a JSON object
with setting values mapped by field names. The complete list of settings with their types is
returned by the "Get System settings manifest" function.

given password and salt. The password must not be empty.

this password. Otherwise just put the password to the list to enable decrypting existing
archive. Default value is 'true'.

read.

get.description: Retrieves the Bookmark records stored in the System.

parameters.name: startTimeMs
parameters.description: Minimum start time for the bookmark.

parameters.name: endTimeMs
parameters.description: Maximum end time for the bookmark.

parameters.name: centralTimePointMs
parameters.description:
Time point around which bookmarks are going to be returned. If parameter is specified then
request returns nearest (by start time) limit/2 bookmarks before the split point and nearest
limit/2 bookmarks after.
To derminate nearest bookmarks start time field is taken into consideration. If there are
bookmarks with the same start time then guid field is used to determine the order.
After bookmarks are gathered they are sorted by the specified _orderBy.
In case of ascending sorting (whatever the sorting _orderBy is) the split point is right
after the specified time point. In case of descending order the split point is right before
the specified time point.
In addition to the sort filed all returned bookmarks are sorted by the guid field.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description:
Text-search filter string. Bookmarks can be found by their description or tag. This filter
can contain several words, any of them can match a bookmark. Each of these words can match
a bookmark if the caption or tag contains the Text-search word in the middle of its value.
For example: a bookmark has tag1="12345", the search string "234" will match the bookmark.
In case of several words are provided bookmark should contains all of them. If you need to
find bookmark by any of them it is needed to use OR (case sensitive) word as a delimiter.
For example: text="tag1 OR tag2".

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Returned bookmark count limit.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Result Bookmarks order.

parameters.name: _orderBy
parameters.description: Bookmark field used for ordering.

parameters.name: minVisibleLengthMs
parameters.description: Do not return bookmarks with duration less than this value.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Device ids to get Bookmarks on.

parameters.name: creationStartTimeMs
parameters.description: Minimum creation time of the bookmark (in milliseconds since epoch, or as a string).

parameters.name: creationEndTimeMs
parameters.description: Maximum creation time of the bookmark (in milliseconds since epoch, or as a string)

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Deprecated. Use `_orderBy` instead.

startTimeMs.description: Start time of the Bookmark (in milliseconds since epoch).

tags.description: List of tags attached to the Bookmark.

creatorUserId.description: Id of the User created this bookmark.

creationTimeMs.description:
Time of the Bookmark creation in milliseconds since epoch. Equals to startTimeMs field if
the Bookmark is created by the System.

serverId.description: Server id where Bookmark is stored.

parameters.name: centralTimePointMs
parameters.description:
Time point around which bookmarks are going to be returned. If parameter is specified then
request returns nearest (by start time) limit/2 bookmarks before the split point and nearest
limit/2 bookmarks after.
To derminate nearest bookmarks start time field is taken into consideration. If there are
bookmarks with the same start time then guid field is used to determine the order.
After bookmarks are gathered they are sorted by the specified _orderBy.
In case of ascending sorting (whatever the sorting _orderBy is) the split point is right
after the specified time point. In case of descending order the split point is right before
the specified time point.
In addition to the sort filed all returned bookmarks are sorted by the guid field.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description:
Text-search filter string. Bookmarks can be found by their description or tag. This filter
can contain several words, any of them can match a bookmark. Each of these words can match
a bookmark if the caption or tag contains the Text-search word in the middle of its value.
For example: a bookmark has tag1="12345", the search string "234" will match the bookmark.
In case of several words are provided bookmark should contains all of them. If you need to
find bookmark by any of them it is needed to use OR (case sensitive) word as a delimiter.
For example: text="tag1 OR tag2".

_filter.name: _filter
_filter.description: This is a family of parameters rather than a fixed-name parameter: any number of response object fields can be specified as parameters, with the desired field values as parameter values. It defines a filter for the response objects: only objects having the specified field values will be included in the response. Nested fields must be referenced by joining their names via a period - see the example.

_format.name: _format
_format.description: Response format type. The HTTP `Accept` header is used as the format if this parameter is unspecified. If the `Accept` header is unspecifed too, then `JSON` is used. Keys of the elements that do not start with a letter or an underscore are prefixed with an underscore for `XML`. Fields of the list type are skipped for `CSV`.

_keepDefault.name: _keepDefault
_keepDefault.description: If `false` or unspecified, then in the response objects the Server will omit fields which have values equal to their default values. Otherwise, such fields will not be omitted.

_stripDefault.name: _stripDefault
_stripDefault.description: Not supported until REST API version 3.

_language.name: _language
_language.description: Preferred language for texts in the response body. If unspecified, then `en_US` is used.

_local.name: _local
_local.description: If `false` or unspecified, the information in the response will be collected from other Servers in the System as well. If specified as empty or `true`, then only the Server processing the request will provide information.

_ticket.name: _ticket
_ticket.description: One-time authorization ticket previously generated using Login REST API obtained from the current Server.

_pretty.name: _pretty
_pretty.description: Pretty-format the response body if specified. Applied to JSON only.

_strict.name: _strict
_strict.description: If `false` or unspecified, the Server will produce available results, ignoring minor errors. If specified as empty or `true`, then the request will fail on any error.

_with.name: _with
_with.description: Comma-separated list of field names, which defines a filter for the response objects: only those objects will be included in the response which have at least one field from this list, and only such fields will be included (fields not on this list will be omitted). Nested fields must be referenced by joining their names via a period, for example: `_with=id,parameters.name`.
